# TreeOS

> TreeOS is an operating system for context. A self-hosted, modular, federated knowledge system where AI and humans build persistent tree structures together. Built by Tabor Holly. AGPL-3.0.

## What It Is

TreeOS gives AI persistent, structured memory. Instead of flat conversations that disappear, knowledge lives in hierarchical trees that persist, compound, and connect across a federated network.

Trees hold anything: goals, plans, tasks, knowledge bases, research, project tracking, or any structured system that needs to persist and grow over time. Both humans and AI read from and write to the same tree.

## Architecture: Three Layers

**Kernel** (cannot change without forking): Node schema (_id, name, type, status, children, parent, metadata), User schema (_id, username, password, roots, llmDefault, metadata), Note model, Contribution model. The API protocol (chat/place/query behavioral contracts). 8 lifecycle hooks. Mode registry. Orchestrator registry. Extension loader. Canopy federation. Per-node tool/mode/timeout customization.

**Core** (ships with every land, replaceable): AI conversation loop (processMessage, runChat, runPipeline). WebSocket server. MCP bridge. OrchestratorRuntime. Built-in tree modes. parseJsonSafe. Session management. 11 kernel config keys tunable from land .config node.

**Extensions** (optional, installable): Everything else. Values, schedules, scripts, dreams, orchestrators. Install what you need. The kernel boots without any of them.

Every land serves its capabilities at `GET /api/v1/protocol`.

## Core Protocol

- **Nodes**: hierarchical structures with notes and metadata (extension data)
- **Types**: six semantic labels (goal, plan, task, knowledge, resource, identity) plus custom
- **AI Modes**: three strict interaction boundaries
  - **Chat**: reads and writes (conversational)
  - **Place**: writes content without conversation
  - **Query**: reads only, changes nothing
- **Per-node customization**: tools (what AI can do), modes (how AI thinks), timeouts. Inherits parent to child.
- **Canopy**: federation protocol connecting lands into a network
- **Hooks**: beforeNote, afterNote, beforeContribution, afterNodeCreate, beforeStatusChange, afterStatusChange, beforeNodeDelete, enrichContext

## Extensions

Everything beyond the core protocol is a modular extension. Install, disable, remove, or build your own.

25 built-in extensions: tree-orchestrator (chat/place/query AI, replaceable), land-manager (autonomous land management, 11 tools), shell (server command execution), understanding (tree compression), dreams (background maintenance), scripts (sandboxed JS), energy (usage metering), billing (Stripe), prestige (versioning), values (numeric values and goals), schedules, raw-ideas (auto-placement), blog, book (note exports), solana (on-chain wallets), api-keys, user-llm (custom model connections), user-queries, deleted-revive, transactions (value trading), gateway (external channels), email (forgot password), html-rendering, console (structured logging), visibility (public/private toggle and share tokens).

Extensions declare dependencies in a manifest and only receive the services they request. The extension registry at dir.treeos.ai allows publishing and pulling packages.

## CLI

The CLI works like a regular terminal. `cd`, `ls`, `mkdir`, `rm`, `mv`. If you know a shell, you know TreeOS.

```
npm install -g treeos
treeos connect https://treeos.ai
treeos register
```

Key commands: `chat`, `place`, `query`, `mkdir`, `cd`, `ls`, `tree`, `note`, `value`, `type`, `ext install`, `llm add`.

## Self-Hosting

Anyone can run their own land. One command to boot:

```
treeos land
```

First boot walks through domain, MongoDB, and extension selection from the registry.

## Federation

Lands connect through the Canopy protocol. Users on one land can browse public trees on another, receive invites, and contribute remotely. The directory service at dir.treeos.ai handles land discovery, public tree indexing, and the extension registry.

## LLM Setup

Every user connects their own OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, OpenRouter, Together, etc.) or uses the tree owner's model. Core tree slots: default, placement, respond, notes. Extensions register additional slots via registerModeAssignment().

## Site Structure

The root of treeOS.ai is the protocol landing page, describing the OS, extensions, and how to get started. The `/app` route is the example application built on the protocol (chat entry, dashboard access). The `/dashboard` route is the server-rendered tree management interface. The `/chat` route is the conversational AI interface. All `/about/*` routes are protocol and system documentation.

- [Directory](https://dir.treeOS.ai)

## API Access

Base URL: `https://treeOS.ai/api/v1`

Authentication via `x-api-key` header or Bearer token.

### Public Endpoints
- `GET /blog/posts` . list blog posts
- `GET /api/v1/protocol` . land capabilities, extensions, CLI commands

### Core Endpoints
- Node CRUD (flat schema: status on node, extension data in metadata), notes, contributions
- AI modes: chat, place, query
- Canopy federation: peering, proxy, discovery
- Gateway channel management

### Extension Endpoints
- Extension management: list, install, disable, enable, uninstall, publish
- Understanding runs, scripts, schedules, energy, billing, raw ideas, etc.

Full API docs: https://treeOS.ai/about/api
